name: Trivy/TeamPCP Supply Chain Attack (March 2026)
description: MITRE ATT&CK mapping for the Trivy/TeamPCP supply chain compromise. Covers initial compromise via imposter commits, malicious binary distribution, GitHub Actions payload, and systemd persistence.

techniques.comment: Stage 1-2: Malicious Trivy v0.69.4 binary distributed via compromised release pipeline + 75/76 trivy-action tags force-pushed with malicious workflow code.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Imposter commits spoofing rauchg and DmitriyLewen pushed to Trivy repo. v0.69.4 tag triggered release pipeline publishing malicious binaries to GitHub Releases, Docker Hub, GHCR, ECR.

techniques.comment: Compromised aqua-bot service account used to publish releases, bypassing normal approval workflows.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Attackers retained access from prior Aqua Security incident, used service account privileges to push tags and trigger automated release pipelines.

techniques.comment: Stage 3: File system sweeps for SSH keys, cloud credentials, tokens, and other secret material on both CI/CD runners and developer machines.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Three-stage payload swept filesystem for .ssh/, .aws/, .gcloud/, .kube/, .npmrc, and other credential stores.

techniques.comment: Stage 3: Memory scraping via /proc/<pid>/mem to extract credentials from running process memory.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Malicious GitHub Action read /proc/<pid>/mem of running processes to extract environment variables and in-memory secrets. This technique bypasses file-based secret detection.

techniques.comment: Stage 2-3: Exfiltration of stolen credentials via AES-256-CBC + RSA-4096 hybrid encryption to typosquatted C2 domain or Cloudflare Tunnel.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Credentials encrypted with AES-256-CBC, key wrapped with RSA-4096, exfiltrated to scan.aquasecurtiy[.]org (primary) or plug-tab-protective-relay.trycloudflare.com (tunnel). Fallback: create tpcp-docs GitHub repos to store stolen data.

techniques.comment: Enumeration of environment variables and system context to identify valuable targets and available credentials.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Malicious Trivy binary collected environment variables including CI/CD tokens, cloud credentials, and service account keys before exfiltration.

techniques.comment: Compromised aqua-bot service account used to publish malicious releases and force-push GitHub Action tags.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Service account from prior Aqua Security incident retained elevated privileges to npm, Docker Hub, GitHub releases, and GitHub Actions.

techniques.comment: Stage 4: systemd user service persistence via ~/.config/systemd/user/sysmon.py polling C2 every 5 minutes for secondary payloads.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: On developer machines, wrote sysmon.py to ~/.config/systemd/user/ as a user-level systemd service. Polls tdtqy-oyaaa-aaaae-af2dq-cai.raw.icp0.io every 5 minutes. Does not require root privileges.

techniques.comment: Forced authentication and token harvesting from GitHub Actions OIDC and CI/CD service accounts during workflow execution.

metadata.name: evidence
metadata.value: Compromised GitHub Actions triggered token exchanges and harvested GITHUB_TOKEN, OIDC tokens, and any secrets mounted in the workflow environment.

metadata.name: source
metadata.value: Wiz Research - Trivy/TeamPCP Supply Chain Attack
